title: A festival I celebrated abroad
summary: Name the country and narrate one moment from a festival you celebrated while travelling.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a festival you attended in another country. Name the country and the festival. Use past simple to narrate what happened. Describe one specific moment and say how it made you feel.
hintStarters: Two years ago, I was travelling in Mexico when I arrived during Día de los Muertos…
I did not know much about the festival before, but when I saw…
That one moment changed the way I thought about…

hintVocab.meaningVi: ngoài vùng an toàn của tôi

sampleText: Three years ago, I travelled to Oaxaca in Mexico and arrived just before Día de los Muertos. I did not plan this — I only found out about the festival when my host family told me. On the first evening, they took me to the town cemetery. I did not know what to expect. When we arrived, the whole place was full of light. Hundreds of candles burned on the graves and people carried orange marigold flowers to place on small altars. The air smelled of incense and food. Families sat next to their loved ones and ate, talked, and laughed together. A woman I did not know gave me a marigold and smiled at me. I thanked her and put the flower on a nearby grave. Nobody asked who I was or why I was there. I felt completely welcome even though I was a stranger. Children ran between the graves and the adults sang old songs. I stood still and looked at everything around me. It was the most beautiful and unusual night I ever had while travelling. I never forgot the feeling of being part of something so full of love.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: arrived just before Día de los Muertos
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đến ngay trước dịp Día de los Muertos

sampleAnnotations.phrase: they took me to the town cemetery
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: họ dẫn tôi đến nghĩa địa thị trấn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the whole place was full of light
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: toàn bộ nơi đó tràn ngập ánh sáng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Hundreds of candles burned on the graves
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Hàng trăm ngọn nến cháy trên các ngôi mộ
mang những bông hoa cúc vạn thọ màu cam

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The air smelled of incense and food
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Không khí mang mùi nhang và thức ăn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Families sat next to their loved ones
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Các gia đình ngồi bên cạnh những người thân đã khuất

sampleAnnotations.phrase: A woman I did not know gave me a marigold
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một người phụ nữ tôi không quen tặng cho tôi một bông hoa cúc

Bọn trẻ chạy giữa các ngôi mộ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the feeling of being part of something so full of love
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm giác trở thành một phần của điều gì đó tràn đầy tình yêu thương

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple narrative: sequencing events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past simple để kể lại một chuỗi sự kiện theo thứ tự: 'arrived', 'found out', 'took me', 'burned', 'carried', 'sat', 'gave'. Mỗi động từ quá khứ đơn đẩy câu chuyện tiến lên phía trước.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple to narrate a sequence of events in order: 'arrived', 'found out', 'took me', 'burned', 'carried', 'sat', 'gave'. Each past simple verb moves the story forward.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Even though vs although
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Even though' và 'although' đều nghĩa là 'mặc dù'. Chúng dùng để nối hai mệnh đề có ý nghĩa đối lập: 'I felt welcome even though I was a stranger' — mặc dù là người lạ, nhưng tôi vẫn cảm thấy được chào đón.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Even though' and 'although' both mean 'despite the fact that'. They connect two clauses with contrasting meanings: 'I felt welcome even though I was a stranger' — the surprise is that a stranger felt welcome.

sampleTranslationVi: Ba năm trước, tôi đến Oaxaca ở Mexico và tới nơi đúng dịp trước Día de los Muertos. Tôi không có kế hoạch trước — tôi chỉ biết về lễ hội khi gia đình tiếp đón tôi kể. Tối hôm đầu tiên, họ dẫn tôi đến nghĩa địa thị trấn. Tôi không biết điều gì sẽ xảy ra. Khi đến nơi, toàn bộ nơi đó tràn ngập ánh sáng. Hàng trăm ngọn nến cháy trên các ngôi mộ và mọi người mang những bông hoa cúc vạn thọ màu cam để đặt lên những bàn thờ nhỏ. Không khí mang mùi nhang và thức ăn. Các gia đình ngồi bên cạnh những người thân đã khuất, ăn uống, trò chuyện và cười nói. Một người phụ nữ tôi không quen tặng cho tôi một bông hoa cúc và mỉm cười. Tôi cảm ơn bà và đặt bông hoa lên một ngôi mộ gần đó. Không ai hỏi tôi là ai hay tại sao tôi ở đó. Tôi cảm thấy hoàn toàn được chào đón dù là người lạ. Bọn trẻ chạy giữa các ngôi mộ và người lớn hát những bài hát cũ. Tôi đứng yên và nhìn mọi thứ xung quanh. Đó là đêm đẹp nhất và kỳ lạ nhất tôi từng có khi đi du lịch. Tôi không bao giờ quên cảm giác trở thành một phần của điều gì đó tràn đầy tình yêu thương.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sequence: 'I ___ to Oaxaca and ___ just before the festival.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs need to be past simple: 'travelled' and 'arrived'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both actions are completed past events.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes the scene on arrival?
options: The whole place were full of light.
The whole place is full of light.
The whole place has full of light.
The whole place was full of light.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The whole place' is singular, so 'was' is correct. Past simple for a past scene.
mcQuestions.hint: 'The whole place' is singular — which past form of 'be' is correct?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct form: 'Hundreds of candles ___ on the graves.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Burned' is past simple of 'burn', correct for describing a scene in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: This is a past scene description — use past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'even though' to show contrast?
options: I felt completely welcome even though I am a stranger.
I felt completely welcome even though I was a stranger.
I felt completely welcome although I was stranger.
I felt completely welcome even I was a stranger.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Even though' is followed by a full clause (subject + verb). Both verbs are past tense.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Even though' needs a full clause: subject + verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple form: 'A woman I did not know ___ me a marigold.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Gave' is the irregular past simple of 'give'.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'give'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means to become part of a group activity?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Take part in' means to participate in an event or activity.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb means to participate?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly narrates placing an object somewhere?
options: I put the flower on a nearby grave.
I putted the flower on a nearby grave.
I was put the flower on a nearby grave.
I have put the flower on a nearby grave yesterday.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Put' is irregular — the past simple is also 'put', not 'putted'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Put' is irregular: present and past simple are the same form.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural way to describe a memorable smell.
options: The air smelled like incense and food.
The air smelled of incense and food.
The air smelled with incense and food.
The air smell of incense and food.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smelled of' is the correct collocation when describing what something smells like (identifying the smell).
mcQuestions.hint: The correct preposition after 'smelled' when identifying a smell is 'of'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'even though' vs 'because'?
options: I felt welcome because nobody knew me.
I felt welcome although nobody knew me, so I was happy.
I felt welcome even though nobody knew me.
I felt welcome although nobody knows me.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Even though nobody knew me' shows a surprising contrast — normally strangers do not feel welcome.
mcQuestions.hint: The surprise is that a stranger felt welcome — which linker shows contrast?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct superlative form: 'It was the ___ night I ever had.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Long adjectives use 'most' for superlative: 'most beautiful', 'most unusual'. Not '-est'.
mcQuestions.hint: Long adjectives use 'most' for superlative, not '-est'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'I ___ the feeling of being part of something special.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Forgot' is the irregular past simple of 'forget'. 'Never forgot' expresses a completed past experience.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'forget'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever attended a festival or celebration in another culture? Did it change the way you thought about that culture? Why or why not?
